operating_ranges.evidence: Any appropriate 0 to +5 V signal of less than 20 kΩ source impedance can be used to supply a set point signal
good contact (typical 0.01Ω or less) to device's case

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Factors affecting zero: Thermal effects and contamination
Using either may nullify regulatory compliance

fault_conditions.name: startup_voltage_jump
fault_conditions.description: Output signal jumps to +4...5V when power first applied
fault_conditions.symptoms: red/green blinking status LED during automatic reset
fault_conditions.evidence: Output signal behavior: Jumps to +4...5V when power first applied

fault_conditions.name: inadequate_stabilization
fault_conditions.description: Signal not stable within millivolts close to zero after warm-up
fault_conditions.symptoms: unstable flow output signal after 15 minutes
fault_conditions.evidence: Approximately 15 minutes after power up - signal stable within some millivolts close to zero

maintenance_actions.name: zero_adjustment_check
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: zero offset due to thermal effects and contamination
maintenance_actions.evidence: Periodically check zero adjustment (e.g., maintenance intervals)

assumptions.statement: Terminals provide standard Sub-D connector interfaces with specific pin configurations
assumptions.reason: Manual provides detailed pin configuration tables for 15-pol and 9-pol Sub-D connectors

assumptions.statement: Proper grounding through connector housing is critical for EMC compliance
assumptions.reason: Manual states 'Connect cable shield to connector housing for grounding' and requires braided shielded cables
